dict.string: Ant (http://ant.apache.org/) is a Java-based build tool.
CMake (http://www.cmake.org/) is an open-source, cross-platform build system.

SCons (http://www.scons.org/) is an Open Source software construction tool—that is, a next-generation build tool. Think of SCons as an improved, cross-platform substitute for the classic Make utility.
Support for the ANTLR (http://www.antlr.org/) parser generator tool.
Write and run your AppleScripts (http://www.apple.com/macosx/features/applescript/) with this bundle. Contains lots of useful snippets.
Syntax highlighting and snippets for Arduino sketches.
Language grammar, snippets, and more for ASP.
Lots of snippets and a language grammar for ASP.NET (http://www.asp.net/)
Support for the Bison parser generator.
Supports one variant of the BBCode markup language (http://www.bbcode.org/).

Language grammar, snippets, and more for the C# language. Which is intended to be a simple, modern, general-purpose, object-oriented programming language. To be executed through an implementation of the Common Language Runtime (CLR). Such as the Microsoft .NET Initiative (http://msdn.microsoft.com/netframework/), Mono (http://www.mono-project.com/) or dotGNU (http://www.gnu.org/software/dotgnu/).
Data interchange format similar to protobuf
A dynamic, functional language designed for building scalable and maintainable applications.
Syntax highlighting for F#, a .NET functional programming language.
Support for the Fortran (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fortran) programming language.

gettext is the GNU Project's toolset for helping programmers and translators to produce multi-lingual messages for software. The toolset can be (and often is) used by java, perl, php, shell and many other languages. This bundle provides syntax highlighting and utilities to help in preparing *.po files, which are text files containing the various messages and their translations
Support for the Go programming language (http://golang.org).
Support for Gri (http://gri.sourceforge.net/), a language for scientific graphics programming.

Support for Haskell (http://www.haskell.org/), a general purpose, purely functional programming language featuring static typing, higher order functions, polymorphism, type classes, and monadic effects.
Syntax highlight for the iCalendar format (http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2445).
Support for Ini(tialization) (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file) / config files (mostly found on MS Windows).
Syntax for writing Installer distribution scripts (http://developer.apple.com/releasenotes/DeveloperTools/Installer.html). Combines XML with JavaScript + custom symbol highlighting.
Objective-C with added iPhone grammar parsing.
Support for the Velocity (http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/user-guide.html) templating language.
Javadoc comment helpers for Java source files.
Support for the jQuery JavaScript library (http://jquery.com/).
Cappuccino (http://cappuccino.org/) is an open source framework that makes it easy to build desktop-caliber applications that run in a web browser.
A bundle for the programming language K. For more info, refer to Kx Systems (http://www.kx.com/).
Support for the LaTeX (http://www.latex-project.org/) typesetting system, the de facto standard for scientific papers.
This bundle supports the LDAP Data Interchange Format and other LDAP related tasks.
Extends CSS with dynamic behavior such as variables, mixins, operations and functions.
Lex/Flex (http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=flex) is a lexical analyzer generator.
Support for the Lilypond (http://lilypond.org/web/) music notation format.
Syntax highlighting for the Limbo programming language used by Inferno.
Support for the Lisp (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lisp_%28programming_language%29) functional programming language.
Support for Logo (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logo_programming_language), the educational programming language best known for its turtle drawing mode.
Logtalk (http://logtalk.org/) is an object-oriented extension to Prolog.
Syntax highlight and a template for the portfile format used by MacPorts (http://www.macports.org/).
Syntax highlight for man pages (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manual_page_(Unix)) and a few commands.

Mathematica is a computational software program used in scientific, engineering, and mathematical fields and other areas of technical computing. It is created by Wolfram Research (http://www.wolfram.com/).

Support for MATLAB (http://www.mathworks.com/products/matlab/), and Octave (http://octave.org), two largely compatible high-level languages that enables you to perform computationally intensive tasks faster than with traditional programming languages.
Equational and rewriting logic specification and programming.
Support for working with Maven (http://maven.apache.org/) based projects.
Support for creating templates for the Movable Type (http://www.movabletype.org/) content management system.
A matrix algebra and optimizer language.
Syntax highlight for functions found in the OpenGL (http://opengl.org/) 3D graphics library.
Structural equation modeling and matrix algebra in the [OpenMx](http://openmx.psyc.virginia.edu) and [umx](http://tbates.github.io) [R](http://cran.r-project.org) packages
This bundle contains language grammar and snippets for PIR programming language (http://parrotcode.org/) from parrot VM.
Support for the Pascal (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pascal_(programming_language)) programming language.
Bundle for the manipulation of RCSB or pseudo-RCSB PDB structure files.
HTML::Template (http://html-template.sourceforge.net/) is a template processing system for Perl.
Support for the Postscript (http://www.adobe.com/products/postscript/) langauge, generally associated with printers.
Processing (http://processing.org/) is an open source programming language and environment for people who want to program images, animation, and sound.

Prolog (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prolog) is a logic programming language. It is a general purpose language, which is especially associated with artificial intelligence and computational linguistics.
Support for the Django web framework (http://www.djangoproject.com/) (Python).
Support for editing your Quake configuration files (http://wiki.quakesrc.org/index.php/Quake%20File%20Formats).
R (http://www.r-project.org/) is a free software environment for statistical computing and graphics. This bundle includes a command that sends current line to the R.app environment or to the Rdaemon.
Ragel (http://www.cs.queensu.ca/~thurston/ragel/) compiles finite state machines from regular languages into executable C, C++, Objective-C, or D code.
A resource description file (http://kevin.sb.org/articles/2006/06/06/the-rez-file-format) is Mac-specific and used to compile the resource fork of a file using the rez shell command.
Template engine for Ruby on Rails.
Ruby on Rails (http://www.rubyonrails.com/) is a full-stack framework for developing database-backed web applications, in Ruby, according to the Model-View-Control pattern.
Template language for easily creating CSS; implemented in ruby.
Support for the Shoulda (http://thoughtbot.com/projects/shoulda) testing framework.
A lightweight templating engine for ruby, more info (http://slim-lang.com).
A general-purpose, multi-paradigm, compiled programming language developed by Mozilla Research.
A type-safe Java VM language incorporating both object oriented and functional programming.
Scheme (http://www-swiss.ai.mit.edu/projects/scheme/) is a statically scoped and properly tail-recursive dialect of the Lisp programming language.
Scilab (http://www.scilab.org/) is a numerical computational package developed by INRIA and ENPC in France. It is similar in functionality to MATLAB and is available to download at no cost.

Syntactically awesome stylesheets (http://sass-lang.com) are translated to well-formatted, standard CSS using the command line tool or a web-framework plugin. This bundle has syntax highlight, lots of snippets, validation, and more.
An object-oriented, dynamically typed, reflective programming language.
Syntax highlight for ssh (http://www.openssh.com/) configuration files.
Swift is a general-purpose programming language developed by Apple and built for safety, clarity, and performance.
SWIG (http://www.swig.org/) is a software development tool that connects programs written in C and C++ with a variety of high-level programming languages.
Support for comma and tab separated values (CSV (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comma-separated_values) and TSV).
Text Adventure Development System, for creating interactive fiction (IF) games.

Tcl (http://www.tcl.tk/) (Tool Command Language) is a very powerful but easy to learn dynamic programming language, suitable for a very wide range of uses. It is often used to write GUI frontends for UNIX stuff.
Terraform syntax highlighting and snippets and such

Textile (http://www.textism.com/tools/textile/) is a lightweight markup language originally developed by Dean Allen and billed as a “humane Web text generator”. Textile converts its marked-up text input to valid, well-formed XHTML.

Txt2tags (http://txt2tags.sourceforge.net) converts text files with minimal markup to HTML, XHTML, SGML, LaTeX, Lout, Man page, Wikipedia, Google Code Wiki, DokuWiki, MoinMoin, MagicPoint and PageMaker.
The scripting language used by the VectorWorks (http://www.nemetschek.net/support/custom/vscript/index.php) CAD software.
Gives support for Verilog including syntax highlighting, code folding and symbols.
Hardware description language used in electronic design automation.
XQuery (http://www.w3.org/TR/xquery/) is a language for querying collections of XML data.

Code completion snippets for Objective-C (http://developer.apple.com/documentation/Cocoa/Conceptual/ObjectiveC/index.html), inspired by the Objective Development code completion dictionary (http://www.obdev.at/products/completion-dictionary/index.html) for Xcode.
Various commands for converting textual outlines (defined via indent) to other formats, such as HTML and LaTeX.
Miscellaneous support for working with regular expressions (http://www.regular-expressions.info/).

Bundle concerning commands, scripts, etc. to deal with general Unicode resp. non-ASCII related tasks such as: Unicode normalizations, conversion to similar characters, Unicode/IPA character inserting, Unicode properties (incl. Japanese and Chinese characters), conversions of Japanese and Chinese characters, highlighting of non-ASCII characters, displaying the character inventory of the current document, etc.

Subversion diff commands that use FileMerge (http://developer.apple.com/documentation/DeveloperTools/Conceptual/XcodeUserGuide/Contents/Resources/en.lproj/04_03_SCMManagingFiles/chapter_28_section_8.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40002687-CJBHDBJD).
For running Objextive-C unit tests using the unit testing framework included with Xcode.
Behavior-driven development (BDD) framework for the Ruby programming language.
Increases the visual font size of section declarations.
Vibrant colors on a deep blue background.
GFM body, heading and raw font styling.
A colorful, friendly theme; originally by Wimer Hazenberg (http://www.monokai.nl/).
